panels.description: Wide shot: A narrow Victorian street in London, foggy and dark. Scrooge, a hunched, thin, sour-faced man in a top hat, walks down the center. Pedestrians actively avoid him, and a blind man’s dog is tugging its owner away into a doorway. High contrast, gloomy atmosphere.

bubbles.text: Nobody ever stopped him in the street... even the blind men's dogs would tug their owners into doorways to avoid him.

panels.description: Medium shot: Inside the counting-house. Scrooge sits at a heavy wooden desk, scowling over ledgers. Through the fogged window, we see the silhouettes of people outside stamping their feet against the cold. The room is dark and claustrophobic.

bubbles.text: It was cold, bleak, biting weather. Scrooge sat busy, keeping human sympathy at a distance.

panels.description: Wide shot: An exterior view of the counting-house office windows at 3 PM. The air is thick with brown, swirling fog. Faint, ruddy candlelight glows from the window panes like smears of blood in the gloom. The neighboring buildings are barely visible phantoms.

bubbles.text: The city clocks had only just gone three, but it was quite dark already.

panels.description: Medium shot: A split view showing Scrooge in his office with a large coal box, and his clerk in a tiny, tank-like cubicle visible through the open door. The clerk is hunched over, shivering in a white comforter, trying to warm his hands over a single, flickering candle. Scrooge stares daggers at the clerk.

bubbles.text: Scrooge kept the coal-box in his own room. The clerk, failing to keep warm, tried to catch heat from his small candle.
